---
title: "Phylo models for fish scaling"
author: "Krista Kraskura"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: false
    code_folding: hide
---

```{r, echo = F, include = FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(formatR)
library(tidyverse)
opts_chunk$set(tidy.opts=list(width.cutoff=80),tidy=TRUE)
```

# Questions and discussion points:

1. What are some common practices in the field of comparative phylogeny
  + What are some example papers that would be a good read? (can be on behavior, morphology, physiology.. etc)
  + What matters the most in context of our work (when the work is not direclty focused on phylogeny itself, should it be phylo-focused?)
2. Is package `ape` robust? Where is `ape` getting the relatedness info? (e.g. genetics sources, sequencing, etc)
3. If we are using a fish phylo tree, is it best to use ALL fish species to make broader inference about the effects of phylogenetics on metabolic scaling? or should we construct the tree for the 93 species we have. 
  + Is our analysis limited because of the **number** of species and the **type** of species we
have?
  + How to address the inconsistent results of _best_ model for differentmetabolic performances and temperatures? (BIC model selection is inconsistent)
  + Is that a problem that our species "cluster". for example: mostly salmonids
4. How to properly analyze the importance of phylogenetics signal versus the effects of temperature, ecology, morphology of species. 
5. From reviews: "The authors have not accounted or adjusted for phylogeny" -  what is the difference between "accounted for" and "adjusted for" in the context of our work.  

# Summary of considered approaches 

|  **General info**           |  **Year**  | **Libra- ries** | **Web links** |
|:-------------------  |:--|:--| :-- |:-------- |
| **1 --** Incorporating Phylo with mixed models by Ben Bolker (developer of `lme4` mixed model package we use for all our original models) | 2017 | `ape`, `lme4` | [Github Notes](https://bbolker.github.io/mixedmodels-misc/notes/phylog.html) |
|**2 --** The Phylogenetic Mixed Model | 2004 | SAS code | [Housworth et al 2004](https://www.journals.uchicago.edu/doi/full/10.1086/380570)  [example use:](https://daijiang.github.io/phyr/)|
|**3 --** A collection of functions to do model-based phylogenetic analysis. It includes functions to calculate community phylogenetic diversity, to estimate correlations among functional traits while accounting for phylogenetic relationships, and to fit phylogenetic generalized linear mixed models.  *It is specifically designed for community data in which species occur within multiple sites (locations).*| 2020 |`pryr`, `ape`, `lme4`,| [Package pryr](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/phyr/phyr.pdf)|
|**4 --** Provides tools for calculating evolvability parameters from estimated G-matrices as defined in [Hansen and Houle (2008)](doi:10.1111/j.1420-9101.2008.01573.x) and fits phylogenetic comparative models that link the rate of evolution of a trait to the state of another evolving trait (see [Hansen et al. 2021 Syst Biol](https://academic.oup.com/sysbio/article/71/5/1054/6448239). The package was released with [Bolstad et al. 2014](https://royalsocietypublishing.org/doi/10.1098/rstb.2013.0255), which contains some examples of use.| 2021| `ape`, `lme4`|[`evolvability`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/evolvability/evolvability.pdf); example publication [Bolstad et al. 2014](https://royalsocietypublishing.org/doi/10.1098/rstb.2013.0255)|
|**5 --** R^2s for Correlated Data: Phylogenetic Models, LMMs, and GLMMs |  2019| `lme4`, `MuMIn`, `rr2` | [Ives 2019 Syst Biol](https://academic.oup.com/sysbio/article/68/2/234/5098616) [Dryad](https://datadryad.org/stash/dataset/doi:10.5061/dryad.345v6)|

<br>



# Steps of the analysis 

## 1. Install libraries

The mixed model packages: `evolvability`, `lme4`
Phylogenetic tree packages: `ape`, `rotl` (for plotting: `ggtree`, `phylobase`, `TDbook`)

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include = FALSE}

# if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#     install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("ggtree")

library(lme4)
library(evolvability) # Almer function
library(ape)
library(rotl)
library(Matrix)
library(pryr) 
library(ggtree)
library(TDbook)
library(ggimage)
library(phylobase)

```


<br>

## 2. Source the metabolic rate datasets 

Ready to use datasets include:

  - RMR warm temperature
  - RMR optimal temperature
  - MMR warm 
  - MMR optimal 
  - AAS warm 
  - AAS optimal
  - FAS warm 
  - FAS optimal 
  - All metabolic rate measurements together

Just a glimpse of what kind of data are available. (Not all columns are shown here)

```{r, echo = FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, inlucde =FALSE}

source("/Users/kristakraskura/Github_repositories/Metabolic-scaling-fish/Codes/Metadata-temperature/JEB/get_scaling_data_temp.R")
source("/Users/kristakraskura/Github_repositories/Metabolic-scaling-fish/Codes/MR-fish-metadata/deltaIC_BICdelta_ggformat.R")
source("/Users/kristakraskura/Github_repositories/Plots-formatting/ggplot_format.R")

data.list<-get_scaling_data_temp(data.amr = "/Users/kristakraskura/Github_repositories/Metabolic-scaling-fish/Data/MR-fish-metadata-data/Fish_AMR_temp_dataset_mar2022.csv",
                                 data.rmr = "/Users/kristakraskura/Github_repositories/Metabolic-scaling-fish/Data/MR-fish-metadata-data/Fish_RMR_temp_dataset_mar2022.csv",
                                 ecology.data = "/Users/kristakraskura/Github_repositories/Metabolic-scaling-fish/Data/MR-fish-metadata-data/Kraskura_species_ecologies_mar2022.csv", 
                                 onlyTop.above = TRUE, save.FishBase.species.data = F, 
                                 exp_rmr = 0.81, exp_amr = 0.81, exp_rmr_warm = 0.84, exp_amr_warm = 0.82,
                                 exp_as = 0.82, exp_as_warm = 0.78)


data.amrAC<-data.frame(data.list[1]) 
data.rmrAC<-data.frame(data.list[2])
data.amrAM<-data.frame(data.list[3])
data.rmrAM<-data.frame(data.list[4])
data.amrER<-data.frame(data.list[5])
data.rmrER<-data.frame(data.list[6])

data.asAC<-data.frame(data.list[7]) 
data.fasAC<-data.frame(data.list[8])
data.asAM<-data.frame(data.list[9])
data.fasAM<-data.frame(data.list[10])
data.asER<-data.frame(data.list[11])
data.fasER<-data.frame(data.list[12])

data.amr<-data.frame(data.list[13]) 
data.rmr<-data.frame(data.list[14]) 
dataMR<-data.frame(data.list[15])
data.as<-data.frame(data.list[16])
data.fas<-data.frame(data.list[17])

# the warm ones 
data.amr.test<-rbind(data.amrAC, data.amrAM)
data.rmr.test<-rbind(data.rmrAC, data.rmrAM)
data.fas.test<-data.amr.test[c(!is.na(data.amr.test$FAS) & is.finite(data.amr.test$FAS)) , ]
data.as.test<-data.amr.test[c(!is.na(data.amr.test$lnAS) & is.finite(data.amr.test$lnAS)) , ]

```

```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr::kable(head(data.rmrER[,c("species", "tempTest", "tempTestK1000", "test_category", "lnBWg",  "lnRMR")] ))

```

<br>

## 3. Use `rotl` and `ape` to get phylo tree with branch lengths 

### Notes about the phylogenetic tree:

- Make sure all species has a match (table at the end of this document)
- Make sure the tree is `class` "phylo" (R formatting)
- Establish phylogenetic trees. 
  + All included species in the dataset (n=94)
  + Species only in RMR dataset (warm and optimal)
  + Species only in MMR dataset (warm and optimal)
  + Species only with aerobic scope data (warm and optimal, AAS and FAS)
- Visualize phylo trees using plots and look at the data. 
<br>

### Notes about the phylo tree methods:

- Needed to clean up and modify the tip labels to make sure **the names in the phylogeny tree and respective matrices match those provided in the metabolic rate (MR) datasets**
  1. For three species, the Fishbase names in the MR dataset are different than the ones provided by the Open Tree of Life (phylo). These species were renamed. (see table below)
  2. For all species, excluded the ott species ID number and the underscore tag to each species name. [Example original: `"Phoxinus_phoxinus_ott1019917"` was changed to `"Phoxinus_phoxinus"`]. Now, the names match those in the metabolic rate dataset and thus can be used in mixed models. 
- Each Phylo tree contains:
  + branch lengths
  + node numbers
  + node tip names (latin names of species)
- Prepared the phylo relatedness data as a matrix that is compatible for mixed models
  + Used `Matrix` package, function `Matrix`. 

<br>

|  **In Fishbase**           |**Open Tree of Life** | **Links** |
|:---    |:--- |:------|
|*Rhinogobius giurinus* | *Rhinogobius similis* | [Fishbase link](https://www.fishbase.se/summary/Rhinogobius-giurinus.html) _*Note*: Issue This species is synonym of Rhinogobius similis Gill, 1859 according to Suzuki et al., 2015 (Ref. 104791). The species page will be removed._ |
|*Pseudobagrus vachellii* | *Tachysurus_vachellii* | [Fishbase link;](https://www.fishbase.se/summary/Pseudobagrus-vachellii.html) [synonyms](https://www.fishbase.se/Nomenclature/SynonymsList.php?ID=50950&SynCode=165984&GenusName=Pseudobagrus&SpeciesName=vachellii) |
|*Tachysurus dumerili* | *Leiocassis longirostris* | [Fishbase link;](https://www.fishbase.se/summary/50980) [synonyms](https://www.fishbase.se/Nomenclature/SynonymsList.php?ID=50980&SynCode=172585&GenusName=Tachysurus&SpeciesName=dumerili) |

<br>

The outputs below are custom-messages that I prepped for my sanity checks. They printed the number of species, and a confirmation that all species are included in the matrix. 

<br>

```{r, message=TRUE, results='hide', warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}

phylo.matrix<-function(species.list, matrix.name, tree.name, dataset.ID){

  taxon_search <- tnrs_match_names(names=species.list, context_name="Vertebrates") 
  ott_in_tree <- ott_id(taxon_search)[is_in_tree(ott_id(taxon_search))]
  tr <- tol_induced_subtree(ott_ids = ott_in_tree)
  
  tr$tip.label <- strip_ott_ids(tr$tip.label, remove_underscores = TRUE)
  labels <- as.data.frame(tr$tip.label)

  labels$`tr$tip.label`[which(labels$`tr$tip.label` == "Oncorhynchus mykiss (species in domain Eukaryota)")]<-"Oncorhynchus mykiss"
  labels$`tr$tip.label`[which(labels$`tr$tip.label` == "Gadus morhua (species in domain Eukaryota)")]<-"Gadus morhua"
  labels$`tr$tip.label`[which(labels$`tr$tip.label` == "Leiocassis longirostris")]<-"Tachysurus dumerili" 
  labels$`tr$tip.label`[which(labels$`tr$tip.label` == "Tachysurus vachellii")]<-"Pseudobagrus vachellii" 
  labels$`tr$tip.label`[which(labels$`tr$tip.label` == "Rhinogobius similis")]<- "Rhinogobius giurinus" 
  tr$tip.label <- labels$`tr$tip.label`
  
  if(all(species.list %in% tr$tip.label)){
    # message(paste(dataset.ID, ": All species names are identified and mathced with phylo data", sep =""))
    message(paste(dataset.ID,": All species names are identified and mathced with phylo data \n",  "N species:", length(species.list), sep = ""))
  }
  
  tr2<-compute.brlen(tr)

  A <- ape::vcv.phylo(tr2)
  tree <- compute.brlen(tr2)
  cor <- vcv(tree, cor = T)
  
  # if((dim(A)[1] * dim(A)[2] == sum(!is.na(A))) && all(colnames(A) == rownames(A))){
  # 
  #   message("The phylo matrix has no missing values, and column/row names has matching species IDs") 
  #   message(paste("Size of the matrix ", dim(A)[1], " X ", dim(A)[2], sep =""))
  #  # The column names of A must be the species identifier. and the same
  # }  
  
  A <- Matrix::Matrix(ape::vcv(tree), sparse = TRUE)

  assign(matrix.name, value = A, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  assign(tree.name, value = tree, envir = .GlobalEnv)

}

data.rmrER<-droplevels(data.rmrER)
data.rmr.test<-droplevels(data.rmr.test)
data.amrER<-droplevels(data.amrER)
data.amr.test<-droplevels(data.amr.test)
data.asER<-droplevels(data.asER)
data.as.test<-droplevels(data.as.test)
data.fasER<-droplevels(data.fasER)
data.fas.test<-droplevels(data.fas.test)

phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.rmrER$species)), matrix.name = "A", tree.name = "tree.kk", dataset.ID = "RMR optimal")
phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.rmr.test$species)), matrix.name = "A.rmr.w", tree.name = "tr.rmr.w", dataset.ID = "RMR warm")

phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.amrER$species)), matrix.name = "A.mmr.er", tree.name = "tr.mmr.er", dataset.ID = "MMR optimal")
phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.amr.test$species)), matrix.name = "A.mmr.w", tree.name = "tr.mmr.w", dataset.ID = "MMR warm")

phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.asER$species)), matrix.name = "A.aas.er", tree.name = "tr.aas.er", dataset.ID = "AAS optimal")
phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.as.test$species)), matrix.name = "A.aas.w", tree.name = "tr.aas.w", dataset.ID = "AAS warm")

phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.fasER$species)), matrix.name = "A.fas.er", tree.name = "tr.fas.er", dataset.ID = "FAS optimal")
phylo.matrix(species.list = unique(levels(data.fas.test$species)), matrix.name = "A.fas.w", tree.name = "tree.fas.w", dataset.ID = "FAS warm")

```

### Making the Phylogenetic Tree!

- This tree below shows species that are in RMR optimal temperature dataset. 

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=10, fig.align='center'}
ggtree(tree.kk) +
  geom_tiplab(as_ylab=TRUE, color='black', size = 7, align = TRUE)

```

<br>

## 4. Mixed models 

- Relying on code and example provided by the developers of `evolvability` package.
  + [The vignette with code of the `evolvability` package](https://github.com/GHBolstad/evolvability/blob/master/vignettes/Phylogenetic_mixed_model.R)
- Ge the sparse matrix containing phylogenetic relatedness data 
  + The example below shows a reduced version of matrix for 5 species only (no space to show full matrix) **the "." is equivalent to zero "0".
  + For visual presention, plotted is also a corresponding example mini phylo tree. 
  + This matrix is added to the `Almer` phylogeny mixed model. 
  
<br>




### Notes on the mixed models (lme4 and Almer)

 Constructed were: 

- Each model in the original (thesis), non-phylogeny format (using `lme4::lmer`)
- The corresponding model incorporating a matrix with phylogeny relatedness (using `evolvability::Almer`)


 Difference in models:

- model sets: Different random effects 
  + set 0:  `(1|species)` = species intercept only
  + set 1: `(1|species) + (1|species:trial)` = species, and nested trial under species intercepts
  + set 2: `(1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial)` = species intercept, nested trial under species intercept, species-specific response to temperature (i.e. different size individuals of the same species, woutl have differe sensititivy to temperature). 
  + set 3: _(models lead to singular fits, these were no longer considered)_
  + set 4: `(1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial)` = species intercept, nested trial under species intercept, species-specific mass scaling slopes 
  + set 5: `(1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial)` = species intercept, nested trial under species intercept, nested species:trial mass scaling slopes
- model types: 
  + mass and temperature affect metabolism independently 
  + (`int`) mass and temperature affect metabolism in interaction 
  + (`.POLY`) nonlinear polynomial Arrhenius temperature response 
  + (`intPOLY`) nonlinear polynomial Arrhenius temperature response that changes with body mass (interaction nonlinear temp X mass)

- Not printed below, but constructed were also models for MMR, AAS, and FAS. BIC comparison for these is shown below.  Specifically compared were: 
  1. best non-phylogeny model (thesis)
  2. equivalent phylogeny model

  
<br> 

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# model set 0
RMR_model0 <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) , data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model0.POLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model0intPOLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model0int <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) , data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)

# model set 1
RMR_model1 <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model1.POLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model1intPOLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model1int <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)

# model set 2
RMR_model2 <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000  + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model2int <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000  + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model2.POLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model2intPOLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2)  + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)

# model set 4
RMR_model4 <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE) 
RMR_model4int <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model4.POLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model4intPOLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)

# model set 5
RMR_model5 <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000  + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model5int <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model5.POLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)
RMR_model5intPOLY <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE)


##  -------- PHYLOGENETICS  MODELS 

Phylo_RMR_model0 <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) , data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model0.POLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model0intPOLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model0int <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) , data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))

Phylo_RMR_model1 <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model1.POLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model1intPOLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model1int <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))

Phylo_RMR_model2 <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000  + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model2int <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000  + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model2.POLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model2intPOLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2)  + (1|species) +(0 + tempTestK1000|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))

Phylo_RMR_model4 <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A)) 
Phylo_RMR_model4int <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model4.POLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model4intPOLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))

Phylo_RMR_model5 <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000  + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model5int <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) +(0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model5.POLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))
Phylo_RMR_model5intPOLY <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg * poly(tempTestK1000,2) + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species:trial) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A))

```




```{r, message=FALSE, include = FALSE }

# RMR warm
RMR_MultReg_W_model1 <- lmer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmr.test, REML=FALSE)
Phylo_RMR_MultReg_W_model1 <- Almer(lnRMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.rmr.test, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.rmr.w))


# Best MMR model and equivalent Phylo
AMR_MultReg_ER_model4int <- lmer(lnAMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.amrER, REML=FALSE)
AMR_MultReg_ER_model4 <- lmer(lnAMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.amrER, REML=FALSE) 
AMR_MultReg_W_model4 <- lmer(lnAMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.amr.test, REML=FALSE) 

# phylo
Phylo_AMR_MultReg_ER_model4int <- Almer(lnAMR ~ lnBWg * tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.amrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.mmr.er))
Phylo_AMR_MultReg_ER_model4 <- Almer(lnAMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.amrER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.mmr.er)) 
Phylo_AMR_MultReg_W_model4 <- Almer(lnAMR ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.amr.test, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.mmr.w)) 



# Best AAS model and equivalent Phylo
AS_MultReg_ER_model4 <- lmer(lnAS ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.asER, REML=FALSE)
AS_MultReg_W_model1 <- lmer(lnAS ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.as.test, REML=FALSE)
#phylo
Phylo_AS_MultReg_ER_model4 <- Almer(lnAS ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.asER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.aas.er))
Phylo_AS_MultReg_W_model1 <- Almer(lnAS ~ lnBWg + tempTestK1000 + (1|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.as.test, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.aas.w))



# Best FAS model and equivalent Phylo
FAS_MultReg_ER_model4 <- lmer(log(FAS) ~ lnBWg + tempTest + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.fasER, REML=FALSE)
FAS_MultReg_W_model2.POLY <- lmer(lnFAS ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTest,2) + (1|species) +(0 + tempTest|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.fas.test, REML=FALSE, control=lmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa",optCtrl=list(maxfun=2e5)))
#phylo
Phylo_FAS_MultReg_ER_model4 <- Almer(log(FAS) ~ lnBWg + tempTest + (1|species) + (0 + lnBWg|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.fasER, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.fas.er))
Phylo_FAS_MultReg_W_model2.POLY <- Almer(lnFAS ~ lnBWg + poly(tempTest,2) + (1|species) +(0 + tempTest|species) + (1|species:trial), data=data.fas.test, REML=FALSE, A = list(species = A.fas.w))

```


<br>

## 5. Model selection using BIC 

```{r, include=FALSE}
RMR_BIC<-BIC(RMR_model0, RMR_model0int, RMR_model0.POLY, RMR_model0intPOLY,
                RMR_model1, RMR_model1int, RMR_model1.POLY, RMR_model1intPOLY,
                RMR_model2, RMR_model2int, RMR_model2.POLY, RMR_model2intPOLY,
                RMR_model4, RMR_model4int, RMR_model4.POLY, RMR_model4intPOLY,
                RMR_model5, RMR_model5int, RMR_model5.POLY, RMR_model5intPOLY,
             
              Phylo_RMR_model0, Phylo_RMR_model0int, Phylo_RMR_model0.POLY, Phylo_RMR_model0intPOLY,
                Phylo_RMR_model1, Phylo_RMR_model1int, Phylo_RMR_model1.POLY, Phylo_RMR_model1intPOLY,
                Phylo_RMR_model2, Phylo_RMR_model2int, Phylo_RMR_model2.POLY, Phylo_RMR_model2intPOLY,
                Phylo_RMR_model4, Phylo_RMR_model4int, Phylo_RMR_model4.POLY, Phylo_RMR_model4intPOLY,
                Phylo_RMR_model5, Phylo_RMR_model5int, Phylo_RMR_model5.POLY, Phylo_RMR_model5intPOLY
             )
RMR_W_BIC<-BIC(RMR_MultReg_W_model1, Phylo_RMR_MultReg_W_model1)

AMR_BIC<-BIC(AMR_MultReg_ER_model4int, AMR_MultReg_ER_model4, Phylo_AMR_MultReg_ER_model4int, Phylo_AMR_MultReg_ER_model4)
AMR_W_BIC<-BIC(AMR_MultReg_W_model4, Phylo_AMR_MultReg_W_model4)

AAS_BIC<-BIC(AS_MultReg_ER_model4, Phylo_AS_MultReg_ER_model4)
AAS_W_BIC<-BIC(AS_MultReg_W_model1, Phylo_AS_MultReg_W_model1)

FAS_BIC<-BIC(FAS_MultReg_ER_model4, Phylo_FAS_MultReg_ER_model4)
FAS_W_BIC<-BIC(FAS_MultReg_W_model2.POLY, Phylo_FAS_MultReg_W_model2.POLY)

```



### BIC RMR - optimal temperatures
- **Best**: Non-phylo `RMR_model5`, phylo model deltaBIC = 38.46
- Truncated version

```{r, echo = FALSE, fig.align = 'left'}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(RMR_BIC)[1:10,]) 
    
# Phylo model can be used in model selection 
```
<br>

### BIC RMR - warm temperatures
**Best**: Phylo
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(RMR_W_BIC))      # Phylo model can be used in model selection 
```

<br>

### BIC MMR - optimal temperatures
**Best**: Non-Phylo
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(AMR_BIC)) 
    # Phylo model can be used in model selection 

```

<br>

### BIC MMR - warm temperatures
**Best**: Non-Phylo
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(AMR_W_BIC)) 
    # Phylo model can be used in model selection 
```

<br>

### BIC Absolute AS - optimal temperatures
**Best**: Phylo
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(AAS_BIC)) 
    # Phylo model can be used in model selection 
```

<br>

### BIC Absolute AS - warm temperatures
**Best**: Non-Phylo
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(AAS_W_BIC)) 
    # Phylo model can be used in model selection 
```

<br>

### BIC Factorial AS - optimal temperatures
**Best**: Non-Phylo ** (low delta BIC)
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(FAS_BIC)) 
    # Phylo model can be used in model selection 
```

<br>

### BIC Factorial AS - warm temperatures
**Best**: Phylo ** (low delta BIC)
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr:::kable(BICdelta(FAS_W_BIC)) 
    # Phylo model can be used in model selection 
```

<br>





## 6. Model summaries comparing best non-Phylo and Phylo model for RMR optimal fishes. 

**Key notes**: 

- The non-phylo model is better (BIC > 7) 
- Slopes are nearly the same between two models (0.81)
- Most difference is in the random effect species-specific intercepts.
- The correlation between temperature effects (independent predictor) and intercept is reduced in Phylo model
- My biological interpretation: the phylogeny does not explain variarion in species-specific slopes, but "absorbs" the variation in species-specific interceps. **More closely related species have more similar RMR**. 
- Also looked at the profile plots. As much as I understand, it would suggest the same take home message about intercepts and species effects (would love to chat more about these!)

<br>
```{r}

#### >>>>  non- Phylo
summary(RMR_model5)


#### >>>> Phylo
summary(Phylo_RMR_model5)
```

<br>

<!-- More information and interpretation of [profile plots](https://lme4.r-forge.r-project.org/slides/2011-03-16-Amsterdam/3ProfilingH.pdf) -->

## 7. Confidence intervals for best models 

The 90% CI were calculated for the best models and the corresponding phylo model (RMR optimal temp only). 

  - sig01, sig02, sig03 is CI for the variance explained by each random effect
    + sig01 = species:trial intercept
    + sig02 = species:trial slope
    + sig03 = species intercept
  - sigma is the unexplained residual (error) variance
  - (Intercept) = intercept (main effect)
  - (lnBWg) = slope (main effect)
  - tempTestK1000 = Arrhenius temp (main effect)
  
```{r, echo = FALSE, message=FALSE, warning = FALSE}


### !!! RUN THIS ONLY ONCE  (long compouting time, save results) --- 
        
        # CI.rmr.ER<-as.data.frame(confint.merMod(RMR_model5, level = 0.90))
        # Phylo_CI.rmr.ER<-as.data.frame(confint.merMod(Phylo_RMR_model5, level = 0.90))
        # CI.rmr.ER$var<-rownames(CI.rmr.ER)
        # Phylo_CI.rmr.ER$var<-rownames(Phylo_CI.rmr.ER)
        # CI.rmr.ER$model<-"regular"
        # Phylo_CI.rmr.ER$model<-"phylo"
        # 
        # CI_table<-rbind(CI.rmr.ER, Phylo_CI.rmr.ER)
        # colnames(CI_table)<-c("CI.l", "CI.h", "var","model")

### !!! RUN THIS ONLY ONCE  (long compouting time, save results) --- 
# VarCorr(RMR_model5)
# write.csv(x = CI_table, file = "./Data/MR-fish-metadata-data/Fish_RMR_temp_phyloCIs_oct19.csv", row.names = FALSE)
CI_table<-read.csv("./Data/MR-fish-metadata-data/Fish_RMR_temp_phyloCIs_oct19.csv")

knitr:::kable(CI_table)

```


1. The plot below shows confidence intervals for each estimated parameter are shown (orange = Phylo models, black = non-Phylo models)
  


```{r, echo = FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

ci.plot<-ggplot(CI_table[ CI_table$var == "(Intercept)", ], aes(color = model))+
  geom_errorbarh(data = CI_table[ CI_table$var == "(Intercept)", ], aes(y = var, xmin=CI.l, xmax=CI.h), linewidth =2, position = position_dodge())+ 
  facet_wrap(.~var)+
  xlim(10, 20)+
  scale_color_manual(values=c('#E69F00','black'))+
  theme_minimal()
ggformat(ci.plot, y_title = "Variable", x_title = "Parameter value range", print = F, title = "", size_text = 8)

ci.plot2<-ggplot(CI_table[ CI_table$var == "lnBWg", ], aes(color = model))+
  geom_errorbarh(data = CI_table[ CI_table$var == "lnBWg", ], aes(y = var, xmin=CI.l, xmax=CI.h), linewidth =2, position = position_dodge())+ 
  facet_wrap(.~var)+
  xlim(0.75, 1)+
  scale_color_manual(values=c('#E69F00','black'))+
  theme_minimal()
ggformat(ci.plot2, y_title = "Variable", x_title = "Parameter value range", print = F, title = "", size_text = 8)

ci.plot3<-ggplot(CI_table[ CI_table$var == "tempTestK1000", ], aes(color = model))+
  geom_errorbarh(data = CI_table[ CI_table$var == "tempTestK1000", ], aes(y = var, xmin=CI.l, xmax=CI.h), linewidth =2, position = position_dodge())+ 
  facet_wrap(.~var)+
  xlim(-5.5, -4.5)+
  scale_color_manual(values=c('#E69F00','black'))+
  theme_minimal()
ggformat(ci.plot3, y_title = "Variable", x_title = "Parameter value range", print = F, title = "", size_text = 8)


ci.plot4<-ggplot(CI_table[ c(CI_table$var == ".sig03"), ], aes(color = model))+
  geom_errorbarh(data = CI_table[ c(CI_table$var == ".sig03"), ], aes(y = var, xmin=CI.l, xmax=CI.h), linewidth =2, position = position_dodge())+ 
  facet_wrap(.~var)+
  xlim(0, 3)+
  scale_color_manual(values=c('#E69F00','black'))+
  theme_minimal()
ggformat(ci.plot4, y_title = "Variable", x_title = "Parameter value range", print = F, title = "", size_text = 8)

ci.plotall<-ggplot(CI_table, aes(color = model))+
  geom_errorbarh(data = CI_table, aes(y = var, xmin=CI.l, xmax=CI.h), linewidth =2, position = position_dodge())+ 
  facet_wrap(.~var, scales = "free")+
  scale_color_manual(values=c('#E69F00','black'))+
  theme_minimal()
ggformat(ci.plotall, y_title = "Variable", x_title = "Parameter value range", print = T, title = "", size_text = 8)



Phylo_RE_rmr<-as.data.frame(ranef(Phylo_RMR_model5))
RE_rmr<-as.data.frame(ranef(RMR_model5))
Phylo_RE_rmr$model<-"Phylo"
RE_rmr$model<-"lme"

randef_RMR<-rbind(Phylo_RE_rmr, RE_rmr)

```


<br>

2. The plot below shows the estimated deviance of each random effects level from the main effect (orange = Phylo models, black = non-Phylo models)

- The random effects of `species:trial` varied at the intercept and slope level. 
- The randome effects of `species` varied only at the intercept level.

**Key note**:

- We can see that the variation at the `species` intercept level is greater in the phylogenetics model compared to non-Phylo model. 

1. **(1|species)**
2. **(BWg | species:trial)**





```{r, echo = FALSE, fig.height = 3, fig.width = 5, fig.align = "center" }

re.plot<-ggplot(randef_RMR, aes(x = term, color = model, group = interaction(model, grpvar)))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(0), lty = "dashed")+
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = term, y=condval), size =3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), pch =21)+ 
  # geom_errorbarh(mapping = aes( xmin=condval - condsd, xmax=condval + condsd), size =0.5, position = position_dodge())+ 
  # facet_wrap(.~grpvar, scales = "free", nrow=3)+
  scale_color_manual(values=c('black','#E69F00'))+
  theme_minimal()
ggformat(re.plot, y_title = "Variable", x_title = "Parameter value range", print = F, size_text = 10)
re.plot<-re.plot + 
  theme(legend.position = c(0.8, 0.2))+
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 7, colour = "black"), 
        legend.title = element_text(size = 7, colour = "black"))+
  guides(color=guide_legend(title="The Random effect"))
re.plot

```


<!-- ```{r, include = FALSE} -->

<!-- prof.mod<-profile(RMR_model5) -->
<!-- Phylo_prof.mod<-profile(Phylo_RMR_model5) -->

<!-- # densityplot(prof.mod) -->
<!-- # densityplot(Phylo_prof.mod) -->
<!-- splom(prof.mod) -->
<!-- splom(Phylo_prof.mod) -->

<!-- ``` -->


## 8. Some more plots with some phylo + ecology and morphology correlates  


```{r, include = FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo = FALSE }
# add Climate, and intercept data to the Phylo tree
# One dataset with species-specific info
species.fas<-read.csv("/Users/kristakraskura/Desktop/BOX/UCSB/Research/Metabolic_scaling/ms-AMR-RMR-Temperature/thesis/data files/species_FAS.csv")
species.rmr<-read.csv("/Users/kristakraskura/Desktop/BOX/UCSB/Research/Metabolic_scaling/ms-AMR-RMR-Temperature/thesis/data files/species_RMR.csv")
species.amr<-read.csv("/Users/kristakraskura/Desktop/BOX/UCSB/Research/Metabolic_scaling/ms-AMR-RMR-Temperature/thesis/data files/species_MMR.csv")

species.fas.w<-species.fas[species.fas$test_category3=="warm", ]
species.fas.er<-species.fas[species.fas$test_category3=="ecol_relev", ]
species.rmr.w<-species.rmr[species.rmr$test_category3=="warm", ]
species.rmr.er<-species.rmr[species.rmr$test_category3=="ecol_relev", ]
species.amr.w<-species.amr[species.amr$test_category3=="warm", ]
species.amr.er<-species.amr[species.amr$test_category3=="ecol_relev", ]

phylo.RE.rmr.interc<-Phylo_RE_rmr[Phylo_RE_rmr$grpvar == "species", ]
colnames(phylo.RE.rmr.interc)[colnames(phylo.RE.rmr.interc) == 'grp'] <- 'species'
RE.rmr.interc<-RE_rmr[RE_rmr$grpvar == "species", ]
colnames(RE.rmr.interc)[colnames(RE.rmr.interc) == 'grp'] <- 'species'

species.rmr.optT<-merge(species.rmr.er, phylo.RE.rmr.interc, by = "species")
colnames(species.rmr.optT)[colnames(species.rmr.optT) == 'condval'] <- 'Phylo-ln(a)'
colnames(species.rmr.optT)[colnames(species.rmr.optT) == 'condsd'] <- 'Phylo-ln(a)SD'

species.rmr.optT<-merge(species.rmr.optT, RE.rmr.interc, by = "species")
species.rmr.optT<-merge(species.rmr.optT, data.rmrER[!duplicated(data.rmrER$species), c("species", "DemersPelag", "BodyShapeI", "Climate", "salintyComb")], by = "species")
colnames(species.rmr.optT)[colnames(species.rmr.optT) == 'condval'] <- 'lme-ln(a)'
colnames(species.rmr.optT)[colnames(species.rmr.optT) == 'condsd'] <- 'lme-ln(a)SD'

species.rmr.optT<-species.rmr.optT[, c("species", "DemersPelag", "BodyShapeI", "Climate", "salintyComb", "lme-ln(a)SD", "lme-ln(a)", "Phylo-ln(a)SD", "Phylo-ln(a)", "slope", "intercept") ]

species.rmr.optT$Climate <- factor(species.rmr.optT$Climate)
species.rmr.optT$salintyComb <- factor(species.rmr.optT$salintyComb)

# species.rmr.optT$species <- factor(species.rmr.optT$species, levels=tree.kk$tip.label)
species.rmr.optT$Newick_label <- species.rmr.optT$species


# p <- ggtree(tree.kk)  %<+% species.rmr.optT + geom_tiplab(as_ylab = TRUE, align = TRUE, offset = .6, hjust = .5)
#     
#     p2 <- p + geom_tippoint(aes(shape = salintyComb, fill = Climate, color = Climate), size = 2) + 
#     theme(legend.position = "right") + 
#     scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 3))+
#     scale_shape_manual(values = c(21,22,23,24,25))+
#     scale_fill_viridis_d(na.translate = F, option = "C")+
#     scale_color_viridis_d(na.translate = F, option = "C")
  

# df <- data.frame(tree.kk$tip.label, species.rmr.optT)

# p0 <- ggtree(tree.kk) + geom_tiplab(align = TRUE, hjust = 0)
# p0 + geom_facet(panel = "Trait",
#                 data = species.rmr.optT,
#                 geom = geom_point,
#                 mapping = aes(x = intercept), stat = "identity") +
#   scale_x_ggtree()

g1 <- as(tree.kk, "phylo4")

# species.rmr.optT !!! MUST HAVE SPECIES IN THE SAME ORDER AS THE TREE
species.rmr.optT$species.node.n <- as.numeric(factor(species.rmr.optT$species))
species.rmr.optT<-species.rmr.optT[order(species.rmr.optT$species.node.n),]
species.rmr.optT$species<-as.character(species.rmr.optT$species)
# [, c("species", "intercept", "slope", "lme-ln(a)SD", "lme-ln(a)", "Phylo-ln(a)SD", "Phylo-ln(a)", "DemersPelag", "BodyShapeI", "Climate")]
rownames(species.rmr.optT)<-as.character(species.rmr.optT$species)

g2 <- phylo4d(g1,species.rmr.optT)
d1 <- data.frame(x = seq(1.01, 1.07, length.out = 3),
                lab = c("Climate", "salintyComb", "DemersPelag"))
```



```{r, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.height = 10, fig.width = 6, fig.align = "center"}

# rename:

phylo.p <- ggtree(g2) +
    geom_tippoint(aes(color = Climate), x = d1$x[1], shape = 15, size= 3) + 
    geom_tippoint(aes(shape = DemersPelag), x = d1$x[3], size=3, color = "#005C83") +
    geom_tippoint(aes(fill = salintyComb), x = d1$x[2], shape = 22, stroke=0, size=3.4, color = "white") + 
    geom_tiplab(as_ylab = T, geom = "text", face = "italic", size = 7.5) +
    xlim(0, 1.08) +
    theme(legend.position = c(0.15, .78)) +
    vexpand(.05, -1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("#00C9DF","#A14000", "#005569",  "#FF8D3E"), 
                       name = "Climate")+
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#55BF81", "#8EF8B6", "#005C83", "#00A4E5", "#9479E0"),
                      breaks=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater; brackish", "Marine", "Marine; brackish", "Marine; freshwater; brackish"),
                      labels=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater-brackish", "Marine", "Marine- brackish", "All"),
                      name = "Salinity")+
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(7, 15, 0, 5),
                       breaks=c("benthopelagic", "demersal", "pelagic", "reef-associated"),
                       labels=c("Benthopelagic", "Demersal", "Pelagic", "Reef-assoc."), 
                       name = "Lifestyle")+
    theme(legend.key = element_rect(size = 6),
        legend.key.height = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        legend.key.width = unit(0.2, "cm"))
phylo.p

# "Mylopharyngodon piceus"


```


In this plot below shows the how the random effects species intercpets compared between the two RMR-optimal models (non-Phylo _vs._ Phylo): 

- The colors focus on **intercepts** + **Climate** and **Salinity habitat** 
- (left side) Big panel directly compares how random-effect estimated intercepts differ between lme (the non-Phylo) and the Phylo model 
  + colors are **Climate** (top) or  **Salinity habitat** (bottom)
  + dots and lines = species
- (rigt side) The small panels show intercepts that were estimated using simple models on species subset only. (each dot is a species)


```{r, fig.height = 7, fig.width = 6, fig.align = "center", message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo = FALSE}
library(reshape2)

species.rmr.optT.long<-melt(species.rmr.optT, 
        id.vars = c("species", "DemersPelag", "BodyShapeI", "Climate", "salintyComb", "slope", "Newick_label", "species.node.n"),
        measure.vars = c("lme-ln(a)", "Phylo-ln(a)", "intercept"), 
        variable.name = "intercept.t", value.name = "value")

sp1_rmr<-ggplot(data=species.rmr.optT.long[!c(species.rmr.optT.long$intercept.t == "intercept"), ], aes(x=intercept.t, y=value, color = Climate))+
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, size=0.5 ,mapping = aes(group = species)) +
  geom_point(pch=19, size=1, position = position_dodge(width = 0.1))+
  theme_classic()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#00C9DF","#A14000", "#005569",  "#FF8D3E"), 
                       name = "Climate")+
  ylim(-8.5, 4)+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", color = "black", size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = unit(c(5.5, 5.8, 5.5, 5.5), "pt"),
        text=element_text(size=20,  family="Arial"))
ggformat(sp1_rmr, y_title = "Intercept value (ln(a))", x_title = "Mixed model: random intercept species",
         print = F,  title = "", size_text = 8)
sp1_rmr <- sp1_rmr + theme(legend.position = "top")

sp1.indep_rmr<-ggplot(data=species.rmr.optT.long[c(species.rmr.optT.long$intercept.t == "intercept"), ], aes(x=intercept.t, y=value, fill = Climate, color = Climate))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.1, width = 0.4, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(pch=19, size=1, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), show.legend = FALSE)+
  theme_classic()+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#00C9DF","#A14000", "#005569",  "#FF8D3E"), 
                       name = "Climate")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#00C9DF","#A14000", "#005569",  "#FF8D3E"), 
                       name = "Climate")+
  ylim(-8.5, 4)+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", color = "black", size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = unit(c(5.5, 5.8, 5.5, 5.5), "pt"),
        text=element_text(size=20,  family="Arial"))
ggformat(sp1.indep_rmr, y_title = "Intercept value (ln(a))", x_title = "Independent species model",
         print = F, title = "", size_text = 8)
sp1.indep_rmr <- sp1.indep_rmr + theme(legend.position = "none")

sp2_rmr<-ggplot(data=species.rmr.optT.long[!c(species.rmr.optT.long$intercept.t == "intercept"), ], aes(x=intercept.t, y=value, color = salintyComb))+
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, size=0.5, mapping = aes(group = species)) +
  geom_point(pch=19, size=1, position = position_dodge(width = 0.1))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#55BF81", "#8EF8B6", "#005C83", "#00A4E5", "#9479E0"),
                      breaks=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater; brackish", "Marine", "Marine; brackish", "Marine; freshwater; brackish"),
                      labels=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater-brackish", "Marine", "Marine- brackish", "All"),
                      name = "Salinity")+
  theme_classic()+
  ylim(-8.5, 4)+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", color = "black", size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = unit(c(5.5, 5.8, 5.5, 5.5), "pt"),
        text=element_text(size=20,  family="Arial"))
ggformat(sp2_rmr, y_title = "Intercept value (ln(a))", x_title = "Mixed model: random intercept species",
         print = F, size_text = 8)
sp2_rmr <- sp2_rmr + theme(legend.position = "top")

sp2.indep_rmr<-ggplot(data=species.rmr.optT.long[c(species.rmr.optT.long$intercept.t == "intercept"), ], aes(x=intercept.t, y=value, color = salintyComb, fill = salintyComb))+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.1, width = 0.4, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(pch=19, size=1, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#55BF81", "#8EF8B6", "#005C83", "#00A4E5", "#9479E0"),
                      breaks=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater; brackish", "Marine", "Marine; brackish", "Marine; freshwater; brackish"),
                      labels=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater-brackish", "Marine", "Marine- brackish", "All"),
                      name = "Salinity")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#55BF81", "#8EF8B6", "#005C83", "#00A4E5", "#9479E0"),
                      breaks=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater; brackish", "Marine", "Marine; brackish", "Marine; freshwater; brackish"),
                      labels=c("Freshwater", "Freshwater-brackish", "Marine", "Marine- brackish", "All"),
                      name = "Salinity")+
  theme_classic()+
  ylim(-8.5, 4)+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", color = "black", size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = unit(c(5.5, 5.8, 5.5, 5.5), "pt"),
        text=element_text(size=20,  family="Arial"))
ggformat(sp2.indep_rmr, y_title = "Intercept value (ln(a))", x_title = "Independent species model",
         print = F, size_text = 8)
sp2.indep_rmr <- sp2.indep_rmr + theme(legend.position = "none")


cowplot::plot_grid(sp1_rmr, sp1.indep_rmr, sp2_rmr, sp2.indep_rmr, nrow =2, ncol=2, rel_widths = c(2, 1))

```

<!-- **Random effects:** -->
<!-- Each random effect is and individual-level effect addressing a question: how much does any random level differs from the population (the fixed effect)?  -->

<!--   1. `1|species` = species-specific difference of the scaling *intercept* (plotted above) -->
<!--   2. `1|species:trial`= nested [trial in species] - specific difference in *intercept* -->
<!--   3. `0 + lnBWg |species:trial` = nested [trial in species] - specific difference in *slope* -->


# Analysis: take Home messages

<br>

1. Is best model with or without phylogeny matrix? Table below summarizes it. Bolded are the cases when phylogenetics models were better than the non-Phylo models (based on delta BIC). If the delta BIC was > 10, no delta BIC notes are added. 

**Take-away**: it is not consistent, whether the best models were those with or without phylogenetics. It depended on the model structure (random effect structure, specifically).

## Table: phylo or non phylo is best model?

|  **MR** |  **Temp.**  | **Best Model** |
|:-   |:-|:---|
|RMR    |optimal    |Non-Phylo      |
|**RMR**    |**warm**       |**Phylo**      |    
|MMR    |optimal    |Non-Phylo      |
|MMR    |warm       |Non-Phylo      |
|**AAS**    |**optimal**    |**Phylo** (deltaBIC = 8.28) | 
|AAS    |warm       |Non-Phylo     |
|FAS    |optimal    |Non-Phylo (deltaBIC < 2)   |
|**FAS**    |**warm**       |**Phylo** (deltaBIC < 2)   |  


2. The effects of phylogeny on scaling parameters (model with or without relatedness matrix)

Visual represeantation of the scaling relationships differences between **non phylogenetics model** (black), and **Phylogenetics model** (orange). _sidenote_: the intercepts change with temperature, they are adjusted for visual presentation)


```{r, echo=FALSE,  out.width = '100%'}
knitr::include_graphics("/Users/kristakraskura/Github_repositories/Metabolic-scaling-fish/Codes/Metadata-temperature/Phylogenetics/Phyo_not_phylo_plot_oct192022.png")
```

## Summary Tables 

### RMR
|  **Metabolic perform- ance** |  **Temper- ature**  | **regular / Phylo ** | **slope** | **intercept** | **Notes**|
|:-   |:-- |:-- |:--- |:---| :----|
|RMR    |optimal    |Non-Phylo  |0.811 se 0.015 | 15.895 se **0.477**| slope variation: nested **species:trial**  |
|RMR    |optimal    |Phylo      |0.813 se 0.015 | 15.334 se **1.016**| slope variation: nested **species:trial**  |
|RMR    |warm       |Non-Phylo  |0.840 se 0.017 | 20.264 se 1.07 | no random slope variation |
|RMR    |warm       |Phylo      |0.840 se 0.017 | 20.515 se 1.24 | no random slope variation |

### MMR
|  **Metabolic perform- ance** |  **Temper- ature**  | **regular / Phylo ** | **slope** | **intercept** | **Notes**|
|:-   |:-- |:-- |:--- |:---| :----|
|MMR    |optimal    |Non-Phylo  |size X temp: -0.373 se 0.09 | 4.14 se 1.21| slope variation: **species**| 
|MMR    |optimal    |Phylo      |size X temp: -0.374 se 0.09 | 4.24 se 1.21| slope variation: **species**|
|MMR    |warm       |Non-Phylo  |**0.820** se 0.039 | 6.89 se 1.01 | slope variation: **species**|      
|MMR    |warm       |Phylo      |**0.752** se 0.110  | 7.02 se 1.01  | slope variation: **species**|     

### AAS
|  **Metabolic perform- ance** |  **Temper- ature**  | **regular / Phylo ** | **slope** | **intercept** | **Notes**|
|:-   |:-- |:-- |:--- |:---| :----|
|AAS    |optimal    |Non-Phylo  | **0.824** se 0.03 | 7.54 se 0.99| slope variation: **species**| 
|AAS    |optimal    |Phylo      | **0.748** se 0.10  | 7.45 se 0.99  |slope variation: **species**| 
|AAS    |warm       |Non-Phylo  | 0.782 se 0.032 | -4.12 se 1.90 | no random slope variation |    
|AAS    |warm       |Phylo      | 0.809 se 0.03  | -5.04 se 2.04| no random slope variation |

### FAS
|  **Metabolic perform- ance** |  **Temper- ature**  | **regular / Phylo ** | **slope** | **intercept** | **Notes**|
|:-   |:-- |:-- |:--- |:---| :----|
|FAS    |optimal    |Non-Phylo  | **-0.012** se 0.017 | 2.06 se 0.087 |slope variation: **species**| 
|FAS    |optimal    |Phylo      | **-0.043** se 0.051 | 2.06 se 0.084 |slope variation: **species**| 
|FAS    |warm       |Non-Phylo  | 3rd order poly fits  | -- | slope variation: **temperature**| 
|FAS    |warm       |Phylo      | 3rd order poly fits  | -- | slope variation: **temperature**|


<br>

**NOTE: This document shows the flow of analysis for RMR at optimal temperatures**

<br>



<!-- ## EXTRA: The table for taxon search. This is an output from `ape` package, function. All our study species are included.  -->

<!-- <br> -->

<!-- ```{r echo=, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE} -->
<!-- knitr::kable(tnrs_match_names(names=unique(levels(dataMR$species)), context_name="Vertebrates")) -->

<!-- ``` -->

`
